title: Is Competition Learned or Innate
subtitle: 竞争是天性还是文化塑造？人类学的答案

paragraphs.en: Psychologists have long been in disagreement as to whether competition is a learned or a genetic component of human behavior. Whatever it is, you cannot but recognize the effect competition is exerting in academics and many other areas of contemporary life. Psychologically speaking, competition has been seen as an inevitable consequence of human drives. According to Sigmund Freud, humans are born screaming for attention and full of organic drives for fulfillment in various areas. Initially, we compete for the attention of our parents.
paragraphs.zh: 长期以来，心理学家对于竞争究竟是人类行为中后天习得的部分还是遗传成分一直存在分歧。无论答案如何，你都无法否认竞争在学术及当代生活诸多领域所施加的影响。从心理学角度看，竞争一直被视为人类内驱力的必然结果。根据西格蒙德·弗洛伊德的观点，人类生来就哭喊着寻求关注，并充满在各方面获得满足的本能驱力。最初，我们为赢得父母的关注而竞争。

paragraphs.en: Thereafter, we are at the mercy of a battle between our base impulses for self-fulfillment and social and cultural norms which prohibit pure indulgence. Current work in anthropology has suggested, however, that this view of the role of competition in human behavior may be incorrect. Thomas Hobbes, one of the great philosophers of the seventeenth century, is perhaps best remembered for his characterization of the "natural world", that is, the world before the imposition of the will of humanity, as being "nasty, brutish, and short."
paragraphs.zh: 此后，我们便受制于追求自我实现的本能冲动与禁止纯粹放纵的社会文化规范之间的斗争。然而，人类学当前的研究表明，这种关于竞争在人类行为中作用的观点可能并不正确。十七世纪伟大哲学家托马斯·霍布斯最为人铭记的，或许是他将“自然世界”——即人类意志施加影响之前的世界——描述为“肮脏、野蛮且短暂的”。

paragraphs.en: This image of the pre-rational world is still widely held, reinforced by Charles Darwin's highly influential work, The Origin of Species, which established the doctrine of natural selection. This doctrine, which takes for granted that those species best able to adapt to and master the natural environment in which they live will survive, has suggested that the struggle for survival is an inherent human trait which determines a person's success. Darwin's theory has even been summarized as "survival of the fittest" — a phrase Darwin himself never used — further highlighting competition's role in success.
paragraphs.zh: 这种关于前理性世界的图景至今仍被广泛接受，并因查尔斯·达尔文极具影响力的著作《物种起源》而得到强化，该书确立了自然选择学说。这一学说理所当然地认为，那些最能适应并掌控其所处自然环境的物种将得以生存，它暗示为生存而奋斗是决定一个人成功与否的固有人类特质。达尔文的理论甚至被概括为“适者生存”——一个达尔文本人从未使用过的短语——这进一步凸显了竞争在成功中的作用。

paragraphs.en: As it has often been pointed out, however, there is nothing in the concept of natural selection that suggests that competition is the most successful strategy for "survival of the fittest." Darwin asserted in The Origin of Species that the struggles he was describing should be viewed as metaphors and could easily include dependence and cooperation.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，正如人们经常指出的那样，自然选择的概念中并无任何内容表明竞争是“适者生存”最成功的策略。达尔文在《物种起源》中断言，他所描述的斗争应被视为隐喻，并且完全可以包含依赖与合作。

paragraphs.en: Many studies have been conducted to test the importance placed on competition as opposed to other values, such as cooperation — by various cultures, and generally conclude that Americans uniquely praise competition as natural, inevitable, and desirable. In 1937, the world-renowned anthropologist Margaret Mead published Cooperation and Competition among Primitive Peoples, based on her studies of several societies that did not prize competition, and, in fact, seemed at times to place a negative value on it. One such society was the Zuni Indians of Arizona, and they, Mead found, valued cooperation far more than competition.
paragraphs.zh: 许多研究对不同文化中竞争相对于合作等其他价值观的重要性进行了检验，并普遍得出结论认为，唯独美国人将竞争推崇为自然的、不可避免的且值得追求的。1937年，世界著名人类学家玛格丽特·米德出版了《原始民族间的合作与竞争》一书，该书基于她对几个并不推崇竞争、事实上有时还贬抑竞争的社会的研究。亚利桑那州的祖尼印第安人就是这样一个社会，米德发现他们重视合作远胜于竞争。

paragraphs.en: After studying dozens of such cultures, Mead's final conclusion was that competitiveness is a culturally created aspect of human behavior, and that its prevalence in a particular society is relative to how that society values it.
paragraphs.zh: 在研究了数十种此类文化之后，米德的最终结论是，竞争力是人类行为中由文化创造的一个方面，它在特定社会中的普遍程度与该社会对其重视程度相关。
